plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQ to 15% to slightly reduce broad index exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and stability.
Buy XLF at 5% to diversify into a quality financial sector.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a realistic target of ~1.5% per month. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Drawdowns should be lower, but growth potential is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its sell flag and leveraged nature.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce overall leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce SMH exposure to 10% as it is in a downtrend.
Buy DDOG at 7% as a strong scanner buy setup.
Buy NET at 5% as a strong scanner accumulate setup to diversify tech.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month with a risk-first growth approach. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed. It blends broad index exposure with quality tech, aiming for moderate growth with controlled drawdowns.

moves.note: Watch for entry on high-score setup

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its sell flag and focus on working setups.
Trim QQQ to 15% to free up capital for higher conviction plays.
Reduce SMH exposure to 10% as it is in a downtrend.
Buy DDOG at 10% as a high-conviction scanner buy setup.
Watch ZS for entry at 8% as another high-scoring setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by leaning into high-scoring scanner setups. This approach raises both upside and drawdown potential. Leveraged ETFs like SOXL are exited due to their sell flag, but overall exposure to strong tech is increased. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the higher risk and potential for rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reallocate towards income-generating assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 5% to minimize high-beta single name exposure.
Sell SMH entirely due to its downtrend and high-beta nature.
Increase QQQI significantly to 40% to maximize income generation.
Prioritize stable distributions and low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income plan aims for ~1% per month by rotating into income-focused ETFs. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable distributions over growth. While designed for stability, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in market selloffs, meaning it is not a savings account.
